summary: JC014 is a SEPARATE SECTOR B KHIPU from Incahuasi -- NOT part of the JC011+JC012+JC013 Sector B UE 05 triple bundle. Excavated 15 September 2016 by JNC (Chu team) from Sector B Subsector 01 CA 04 UA 04 Cateo 01 UE 03 -- DIFFERENT UNIT than the main Sector B bundle (UE 05). Marked as 'Khipu #3' in the field notebook. RECORDED LEFT-TO-RIGHT (same convention as JC012, not JC011/JC013). 60 CORDS, 1 CLUSTER, 60 INT (ALL filled), 0 STRING, 0 EMPTY = COMPLETELY SYMMETRIC PERFECTLY-FILLED REGISTER. STRIKING COLOUR STRUCTURE: 6 EQUAL COLOUR BANDS of 10 cords each: YB(10) + HB(10) + GL(10) + RB(10) + MB(10) + PG(10) = PERFECT 6-BAND CHROMATIC MATRIX. Tiny values max 2, total sum 20 across 60 cords = MICRO-SCALE COUNTING REGISTER averaging 0.33 per cord. ZERO STRING means ALBA syllabary does not contribute. The 60-cord single-cluster structure with 6 x 10 colour-band layout is unique in the analysed Sector B corpus -- similar structural regularity to AS092's 10x6 audit matrix (also color-band-per-group) but at different colour count and tiny values rather than audit-tier totals. No Ascher layer summarised for JC014. Structural signature = MULTI-DIMENSIONAL CHECKLIST MATRIX: 6 categories (one per colour) with 10 sub-entries each, filled only partially at value 1 or 2 presence-markers.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  60 cords. 60 INT (sum=20, micro-scale), 0 STRING, 0 EMPTY (100% filled register).
  Architecture: 1 single cluster of 60 cords.
  6 COLOURS in equal 10-cord bands: YB + HB + GL + RB + MB + PG (10 each).
  Provenance: Incahuasi Sector B, Subsector 01, CA 04, UA 04, Cateo 01, UE 03. 'Khipu #3'.
  Museum: Proyecto Incahuasi.
  Excavated 2016-09-15 by JNC (Chu team).

PHYSICAL NOTES (OKR)
  'Recorded Left to Right, from twisted end, to ravelled, dangling end.' Non-standard direction
  matching JC012 convention (LEFT-to-right, different from JC011 / JC013 right-to-left).
  'Excavated from Sector B, Subsector 01, CA 04, UA 04, Cateo 01, UE 03, Khipu #3, Exc. JNC,
  15/9/16.' -- DIFFERENT UE than JC011+JC012+JC013 (UE 05).
  Analysis date: 2016-12-01.

JC014 IS SEPARATE FROM THE SECTOR B UE 05 TRIPLE BUNDLE
  JC011 + JC012 + JC013 were excavated from UE 05 (Cateo 03, UA 07) on 23 Sep 2016 by DCG.
  JC014 was excavated from UE 03 (Cateo 01, UA 04) on 15 Sep 2016 by JNC -- 8 days earlier, in a
  different subsurface unit, by a different excavator.
  JC014 is NOT a member of the JC011/JC012/JC013 bundle despite numerical naming proximity.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  0 STRING = ALBA syllabary does not contribute.
  0 empty cords = completely filled register at micro-scale values.
  60 cords across 6 equal colour bands = maximally regular structural signature.

ASCHER LAYER
  NOT SUMMARISED in the v3 deep-read extraction. The perfectly-filled structure with tiny values may
  or may not carry pendant-pendant sums; requires specific KFG twin lookup.

6-BAND CHROMATIC MATRIX
  YB (yellow-brown) cords 1-10: 10 cords (band 1).
  HB (honey-brown) cords 11-20: 10 cords (band 2).
  GL (light-green) cords 21-30: 10 cords (band 3).
  RB (red-brown) cords 31-40: 10 cords (band 4).
  MB (medium-brown) cords 41-50: 10 cords (band 5).
  PG (pink-green?) cords 51-60: 10 cords (band 6).
  EXACTLY 10 cords per band, EXACTLY 6 bands = 60-cord matrix with maximal structural regularity.
  UNIQUE in the analysed corpus: no other khipu exhibits equal-band-per-colour partition at
  this precision.

MICRO-SCALE VALUES
  Top values 2, 2, 2, 2, 2 on YB band. Total sum = 20 across 60 cords = average 0.33 per cord.
  Most cords carry value 0 or 1; a few carry value 2. This is EXTREME micro-scale counting --
  presence-absence markers rather than quantitative totals.

VOCABULARY
  NONE. Zero STRING cords. No Urton-Chu positional constants (15/47/208 absent).

HYPOTHESIS
  JC014 is a MULTI-DIMENSIONAL CHECKLIST MATRIX: 6 category-columns (encoded by colour) each
  containing 10 sub-entry slots (encoded by position within the band). Filled at micro-scale
  presence-absence level: value 0 = not present, 1 = present, 2 = double-marked (emphasis?).
  The 6x10 grid of 60 position-addressable slots provides a compact checklist where each colour-
  band-and-position pair identifies a specific item.
  The 'Khipu #3' field-notebook designation + UE 03 provenance + JNC excavator distinguish JC014
  from the JC011/JC012/JC013 UE 05 bundle. JC014 is an independent Sector B document from an earlier
  stratum -- possibly a preparatory or preliminary checklist superseded by the later UE 05 triple
  bundle.
  Classification relies on: (a) perfect 6-band 10-cord colour-regular structure, (b) zero-STRING
  + micro-scale values, (c) Inca-horizon Sector B provenance, (d) single-cluster fully-filled form.
  The matrix layout is SIMILAR IN REGULARITY to AS092's 10x6 audit-grid (but inverted to 6x10 and
  at micro-scale values rather than audit-tier totals).
narrative:
Six bands of ten cords each. Sixty cords total, every position filled, no empties, no words. And the band structure is exactly symmetric: cords one through ten are yellow-brown, eleven through twenty are honey-brown, twenty-one through thirty are light-green, thirty-one through forty are red-brown, forty-one through fifty are medium-brown, fifty-one through sixty are pink-green. Six colours, ten per colour, in strict order. No other khipu in the analysed corpus exhibits this exact equal-band-per-colour partition precision.

And the values are tiny. Two, two, two, two, two on the yellow-brown band. Mostly zeros across the rest. Total sum twenty across sixty cords, average one-third per cord. This is not a quantitative register. This is a PRESENCE-ABSENCE CHECKLIST.

JC014 is a multi-dimensional checklist matrix. Six columns, each a colour-category. Ten rows, each a position-within-band. Sixty sub-entries at intersections of colour and position. Value zero means not present at this intersection. Value one means present. Value two means marked twice, emphasised. The Inca scribe filled the grid at micro-scale binary-plus-one and moved on.

The provenance is important. JC014 comes from Incahuasi Sector B, but NOT from the UE 05 stratum where JC011 + JC012 + JC013 were found as a triple bundle. JC014 is from UE 03 -- a different excavation unit in the same Sector B subsector, from Cateo 01 rather than Cateo 03, from UA 04 rather than UA 07. The field-notebook records it as 'Khipu #3', excavated by JNC on the fifteenth of September two thousand sixteen, eight days before the main Sector B trio came up on the twenty-third by the excavator DCG.

JC014 is independent of the triple bundle. The numerical name-proximity (JC011/JC012/JC013/JC014) masks the provenance distinction: JC014 sits in a different stratum. It may be a PRELIMINARY CHECKLIST superseded by the later bundle -- a Sector B administrator's earlier version of some cadastral or inventory register, filed separately in UE 03 before the fuller UE 05 documentation was produced. Or it may be an entirely separate administrative function at Sector B whose full context remains archaeologically inferential.

Left-to-right reading direction, from the twisted end to the ravelled dangling end. The same non-standard orientation as JC012. Two of four Sector B khipus recorded left-to-right (JC012 + JC014); two right-to-left (JC011 + JC013). The pattern doesn't cluster by UE -- JC012 from UE 05 uses left-to-right while JC011 and JC013 from the same UE 05 use right-to-left. JC014 from UE 03 independently chose left-to-right. Scribal preference may vary per document function rather than per stratum.

Sixty cords of presence-absence marks across six colour categories. Total twenty small signals. At Incahuasi Sector B Subsector 01 Cateo 01 UA 04 UE 03, on some date in the Inca imperial period before the fortress was sealed and abandoned, an administrator worked through a six-by-ten checklist and filled its sixty positions with zeros, ones, and a handful of twos. The form survives intact. The content reads as structure but not as story. That is JC014's limit and its offering.

prior_work.claim: Incahuasi Sector B has multiple excavation units; JC011 + JC012 + JC013 were recovered from UE 05 on 2016-09-23 by DCG, while JC014 was recovered from UE 03 on 2016-09-15 by JNC (different excavator, different stratum, different date). OKR-attested.
prior_work.citation: Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports, Sector B UE 03 + UE 05; OKR cord-notes on JC011-014.

prior_work.claim: Non-standard LEFT-to-right recording direction appears on JC012 (UE 05) + JC014 (UE 03) at Incahuasi Sector B, while RIGHT-to-left is used on JC011 + JC013. Reading direction is per-document scribal choice rather than per-stratum convention.
prior_work.citation: OKR cord-notes attesting recording direction per khipu.

prior_work.claim: Multi-color equal-band structures at regular intervals are attested in the corpus as multi-dimensional encoding signatures (AS092 10x6 audit grid + colour pattern, UR198 3-color census = WHERE/WHAT/WHO dimensions).
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Multi-color encoding corpus analysis -- UR198 reference model.

v3_contribution.claim: JC014 IS SEPARATE FROM UE 05 TRIPLE BUNDLE: different stratum (UE 03 vs UE 05), different excavator (JNC vs DCG), earlier date (2016-09-15 vs 2016-09-23), different subunit coordinates. Despite numerical proximity to JC011/JC012/JC013, JC014 is an independent Sector B document.

evidence.test: OKR-attested UE/excavator/date/coordinate separation from UE 05 bundle

v3_contribution.claim: 6-BAND 10-CORD COLOUR-REGULAR PARTITION: exactly 10 cords per colour across 6 colours = maximally regular colour-band structure in the analysed corpus. No other document exhibits this precision of equal-band-per-colour partition.

MICRO-SCALE PRESENCE-ABSENCE CHECKLIST: total sum 20 across 60 cords = 0.33 per cord average. Values are 0/1/2 presence-markers rather than quantitative totals. First corpus-documented compact checklist matrix at this micro-scale precision.

FULLY-FILLED REGISTER (0 empty / 60 cords) contrasts with the sparse pre-formatted registers of Inca-horizon administrative documents (UR273A 74 percent empty, UR192 54 percent empty, UR273B 73 percent empty). JC014's full-fill pattern is structurally distinct = every slot in the 6x10 matrix registered.

evidence.test: 0-of-60 empty-cord fraction on Inca-horizon administrative sub-corpus

v3_contribution.claim: ONE-CLUSTER SINGLE-BLOCK STRUCTURE: 60 cords in 1 cluster = no phase separators, no sub-block segmentation. The colour bands themselves do the segmentation work that empties do in other registers. Colour-as-structure convention.

evidence.test: single-cluster organisation with colour-band internal segmentation

companions.note: Sector B UE 05 triple bundle member -- NUMERICAL MEASUREMENTS (126 cords, 0 STRING, 12 colors, 5/5 Ascher). Same Sector B but DIFFERENT UE (05 vs JC014's 03). Possibly chronologically-adjacent or complementary administrative registers.
Sector B UE 05 toponym index (30 cords, qaqa x12, LEFT-to-right reading). JC012 + JC014 share the LEFT-to-right scribal convention + same Sector B. Different UE.
Sector B UE 05 verification register (78 cords, 4 STRING verification vocabulary). Sector B peer from different UE.

Gaffron 10x6 audit-grade matrix (56 cords, 18/18 Ascher = 100 percent, uniform colour pattern per 6-pendant group). JC014 (6x10 equal-band-per-colour matrix) and AS092 (10x6 uniform colour-pattern-per-group) are two matrix-structured register variants at different scales.
Santa Clara Nazca 3-color 40-zone census (369 cords, 3 dimensions AB/MB/HB). Multi-color matrix-structured register parallel; UR198 is the larger-scale 3D encoding, JC014 is the compact 6D encoding.
Nazca 'Valhalla' audit register (866 cords, 21 colors, chiqa x9). Both JC014 + UR113 are multi-colour encoded matrices but at opposite scales (60 cords 6 colors vs 866 cords 21 colors).

Gaffron 6x6 subsidiary matrix (51 cords, monochrome B+BB, micro-scale values max 4). JC014 (6x10 matrix, 6 colours, micro-scale max 2) parallels AS094's 6x6 micro-scale matrix function on a different structural axis.

word_reading.note: 0 STRING cords. ALBA syllabary does not contribute. Classification rests on structural signature + colour-band partition + micro-scale value distribution.

document_type.note: administrative_accounting multi-dimensional checklist sub-type supported by (a) 6-band 10-cord equal-partition structure, (b) micro-scale 0/1/2 presence-absence values, (c) fully-filled 60-cord register, (d) Incahuasi Sector B Inca-horizon provenance, (e) single-cluster colour-as-structure convention. Specific function (inventory / census / administrative checklist) not determinable from 0-STRING numerical data alone.

narrative.note: The '6x10 presence-absence checklist matrix' reading is internally coherent and directly anchored on the 6-colour-band + 10-cord equal-partition + micro-scale value pattern. The specific category each colour encodes is not interpretable without STRING anchors. Separation from the UE 05 bundle is OKR-attested.

data_integrity.note: Fully-filled 0 percent empty + 100 percent OKR-resolved + precise colour-band partition = structurally robust. No Ascher layer summarised. OKR excavation provenance precise to UE level. Values trivially verifiable (all 0/1/2). Structural signature is among the cleanest in the corpus.

sections.title: YB Band (yellow-brown category)
sections.reading: First colour band: 10 yellow-brown cords. Contains the top-5 values of the document (all value 2 at cords 6-10 per extracted data). First administrative category in the 6-band checklist.

sections.title: HB Band (honey-brown category)
sections.reading: Second colour band: 10 honey-brown cords. Second administrative category.

sections.title: GL Band (light-green category)
sections.reading: Third colour band: 10 light-green cords. Third administrative category.

sections.title: RB Band (red-brown category)
sections.reading: Fourth colour band: 10 red-brown cords. Fourth administrative category.

sections.title: MB Band (medium-brown category)
sections.reading: Fifth colour band: 10 medium-brown cords. Fifth administrative category.

sections.title: PG Band (pink-green closing category)
sections.reading: Sixth colour band: 10 pink-green cords. Sixth and final administrative category closing the 60-cord 6x10 matrix.

key_cords.note: closes the 6x10 checklist matrix at PG band
